steps.title: Identify the type of fact being stated
steps.content: The sentence states a defining, permanent property: a zinc atom always has 30 protons. This is a timeless scientific fact, not an event in the past or a temporary ongoing action.

steps.title: Apply the simple present tense rule for universal facts, then eliminate
steps.content:
Simple present tense expresses permanent truths and defining characteristics.

A. has had — present perfect; implies the property was acquired at some point in the past, not that it is permanent. Eliminated.
B. had — simple past; implies the property no longer holds. Eliminated.
C. is having — present progressive; implies a temporary ongoing action, not a permanent trait. Eliminated.
D. has — simple present; correctly expresses the permanent, defining property of a zinc atom. Correct.

D is correct.
